frame_analyses.description: Trainee standing at trainer box with both hands visible. Six rubber pegs visible on left peg tower. Instruments not yet grasped.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting position established, good ergonomic stance

frame_analyses.description: Left hand (non-dominant) grasping first peg using left needle driver. Peg positioned at top of left tower.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean grasp at midpoint of peg. Good instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: First peg elevated and transferred in midair from left to right side. Right hand (dominant) needle driver positioned to receive.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transfer trajectory. Peg maintained centered between instruments.

frame_analyses.description: First peg placed on right peg tower using right needle driver. Peg seated on rightmost peg.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled placement. Peg properly seated on peg post.

frame_analyses.description: Second peg being grasped by left needle driver from left tower. Multiple pegs remain on left tower.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent grasp technique. Proper instrument positioning.

frame_analyses.description: Multiple pegs visible transferred midair. Third or fourth peg in transfer sequence. Right side tower accumulating pegs.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Rhythm established. Efficient instrument exchanges visible.

frame_analyses.description: Final pegs from left tower being transferred. Left tower now mostly empty with only 1-2 pegs remaining.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintaining consistent technique through fatigue zone.

frame_analyses.description: Reverse phase initiated. Right hand (now non-dominant for reverse) grasping pegs from right tower for return to left.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean transition to reverse direction. Right tower pegs being picked up systematically.

frame_analyses.description: Pegs being transferred back from right to left in reverse sequence. Multiple midair transfers visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand coordination during reverse phase. Smooth instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: Final pegs being placed back on left tower. Right tower now empty or nearly empty.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing task systematically. All pegs appearing to reach final destination.

frame_analyses.description: Task nearing completion. All six pegs visible on left tower in final configuration. Instruments withdrawn.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion. All pegs on correct tower.

penalties.description: No dropped pegs outside field visible. No pegs missing from final placement. All pegs successfully transferred and returned.

confidence_rationale: Completion time clearly visible from frame progression. All six pegs successfully transferred forward and reverse with no observable drops or placement errors. Camera angles provide consistent view of both peg towers throughout task. Minor uncertainty in exact moment of task completion due to frame-by-frame estimation, but no significant ambiguity in scoring components.
cannot_determine: Exact frame-to-second correspondence without explicit video timestamp data
Whether trainee had prior FLS experience from video alone
technique_summary: Competent intermediate performance. Trainee demonstrates solid fundamentals with consistent instrument control, smooth peg transfers, and successful completion of all six pegs in both directions. Completion time of 108 seconds falls in intermediate range (60–120s benchmark). No penalties incurred. Task execution shows developing efficiency and technique.
strengths: All pegs successfully transferred without drops
Smooth hand transitions between forward and reverse phases
Consistent grasp technique maintained throughout task
Efficient midair transfers with good instrument coordination
No visible wasted motion or hesitation in peg placement
improvement_suggestions: Reduce completion time toward 60–90s range through increased repetition and muscle memory
Maintain same error-free performance while increasing transfer speed
Practice instrument exchanges to further reduce transition time between pegs
